title: 牙医谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\203-牙医谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Inspector Japp and Hercule Poirot discuss the death of the dentist, Mr. Morley. Japp notes that a patient named Mr. Ambrosio left the clinic at 12:25 and reported that Morley appeared calm. A pathologist suggests that Morley was shot before 1:00. Poirot argues that suicide is unlikely, observing that Morley was energetic and orderly as recently as 12:25. Japp also notes that the body's position seems unusual and that marks on the carpet suggest something was dragged.

While examining the scene, Poirot notices a door leading to a small office containing a desk and tea utensils. Japp identifies this as the office of Miss Neville, the dentist's secretary, who is currently absent. Japp lists several individuals who had the opportunity to kill Morley, including his sister, his servants, his partner Riley, a young man named Alfred, or various patients. Poirot emphasizes that they must find a motive, as Morley appeared to be a harmless man.

Poirot and Japp interview Miss Molly George, Morley's sister. She insists suicide is inconceivable, though she mentions her brother was irritable that morning due to a heavy schedule and the absence of his assistant. She explains that Miss Neville, who has worked for Morley for three years, left for Somerset after receiving a telegram stating her aunt had suffered a stroke. Miss Molly suggests that Morley was upset by Neville's absence and speculates that Neville might have actually taken the day off to visit a fiancé whom Morley disapproves of.
